content.text:
Candle v11.2 is extracted to `fox-alien-cnc\candle-11.2\Candle\candle.exe`. Adding an installer script, a Masuter launcher, setup notes, and `.gitignore` entries for the portable app and zip.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
# Downloads and extracts Candle (Denvi/Candle) portable build next to this script.
$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
$root = Split-Path -Parent $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path
$version = "11.2"
$zipName = "candle-$version-portable.zip"
$url = "https://github.com/Denvi/Candle/releases/download/v$version/$zipName"
$zipPath = Join-Path $root $zipName
$outDir = Join-Path $root "candle-$version"

if (Test-Path (Join-Path $outDir "Candle\candle.exe")) {
    Write-Host "Candle already present at: $(Join-Path $outDir 'Candle\candle.exe')"
    exit 0
}

Write-Host "Downloading $url ..."
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $url -OutFile $zipPath -UseBasicParsing

Write-Host "Extracting to $outDir ..."
if (Test-Path $outDir) { Remove-Item $outDir -Recurse -Force }
Expand-Archive -Path $zipPath -DestinationPath $outDir -Force

if (-not (Test-Path (Join-Path $outDir "Candle\candle.exe"))) {
    throw "Extract failed: candle.exe not found under $outDir\Candle"
}

Write-Host "Done. Run Run-Candle-Masuter.bat or: $($outDir)\Candle\candle.exe"

@echo off
setlocal
rem Fox Alien Masuter: GRBL over USB — use CH340 driver, 115200 baud in Candle (Service ^> Settings).
set "HERE=%~dp0"
set "CND=%HERE%candle-11.2\Candle"
if not exist "%CND%\candle.exe" (
  echo Candle not found. Run install-candle.ps1 first.
  pause
  exit /b 1
)
cd /d "%CND%"
start "" "candle.exe"

Fox Alien Masuter + Candle (GRBL)
=================================

Router: GRBL controller, USB serial (often CH341/CH340 — shows as USB-SERIAL CH340 in Device Manager).

1) USB driver (once)
   If Windows does not assign a COM port, install the vendor driver from WCH:
   https://www.wch-ic.com/downloads/CH341SER_EXE.html
   Replug USB and confirm a COM port appears under Ports (COM & LPT).

2) Start Candle
   Double-click Run-Candle-Masuter.bat (or run candle-11.2\Candle\candle.exe).

3) Connect in Candle
   Service > Settings (or connection panel):
   - Port: the COM port from Device Manager (e.g. COM3)
   - Baud rate: 115200 (standard for GRBL 1.1)
   Apply and connect. Status should show Grbl and position updates.

4) Machine / workspace (first run)
   Set soft limits and travel to match your Masuter model in the firmware/Candle as needed.
   Homing: only if you have limit switches wired — use controls carefully.

5) Sending jobs
   File > Open to load NC/G-code, then use the send/run controls after checking origin and Z clearance.

Reinstall Candle: run install-candle.ps1 (downloads v11.2 portable from GitHub).

Official Candle project: https://github.com/Denvi/Candle

Here is what is in place.

## Installed: Candle v11.2 (portable)

- **Location:** `c:\Users\User\Desktop\MEDISOFT\fox-alien-cnc\candle-11.2\Candle\candle.exe`  
- Downloaded from [Denvi/Candle v11.2](https://github.com/Denvi/Candle/releases/tag/v11.2) (`candle-11.2-portable.zip`).

## Integrated for Fox Alien **Masuter** (GRBL)

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| Driver | If there is **no COM port**, install the **CH341/CH340** driver ([WCH CH341SER](https://www.wch-ic.com/downloads/CH341SER_EXE.html)); the Masuter manual calls this out for Candle. |
| Start | Run **`fox-alien-cnc\Run-Candle-Masuter.bat`** (starts Candle with the correct working directory for Qt plugins). |
| Connect | In Candle: **Service → Settings** — set **port** to your **COMx** (Device Manager → Ports) and **115200** baud for GRBL 1.1. |

Longer step-by-step notes are in **`fox-alien-cnc\masuter-setup.txt`**.

## Scripts added

- **`install-candle.ps1`** — Re-downloads and extracts Candle if the folder is missing (e.g. after a fresh clone).  
- **`Run-Candle-Masuter.bat`** — Launches Candle for this machine.

## `.gitignore`

`fox-alien-cnc/candle-11.2/` and the zip are ignored so the ~22 MB portable app is not committed by mistake.

---

**Safety:** Clear the bed, keep a hand near the E-stop / USB disconnect, and only enable **homing** if your Masuter has limit switches configured. If you tell me the exact model (e.g. Masuter 3, 4040, Pro), I can align the **workspace/soft-limit** hints with typical travel for that revision.

CH340 on COM5 — driver reports OK (already installed). Connecting to COM5 and sending small GRBL moves to verify control.

[REDACTED]

input.command:
$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
$com = "COM5"
$baud = 115200
$port = New-Object System.IO.Ports.SerialPort $com, $baud, ([System.IO.Ports.Parity]::None), 8, ([System.IO.Ports.StopBits]::One)
$port.ReadTimeout = 3000
$port.WriteTimeout = 3000
$port.NewLine = "`n"
$port.Open()
Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 1500
$welcome = ""
$t0 = Get-Date
while (((Get-Date) - $t0).TotalMilliseconds -lt 2500) {
  if ($port.BytesToRead -gt 0) { $welcome += $port.ReadExisting() }
  Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 80
}
"--- Welcome/buffer ---"; $welcome

function Read-UntilQuiet([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$p, $msTotal = 800) {
  $acc = ""
  $dead = (Get-Date).AddMilliseconds($msTotal)
  while ((Get-Date) -lt $dead) {
    if ($p.BytesToRead -gt 0) { $acc += $p.ReadExisting() }
    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 40
  }
  return $acc
}

function Send-Line([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$p, [string]$line) {
  $p.DiscardInBuffer()
  [void]$p.BaseStream.Flush()
  $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes($line + "`r")
  $p.Write($bytes, 0, $bytes.Length)
  Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 120
  return (Read-UntilQuiet $p 900)
}

$r = Send-Line $port '$X'
"Unlock `$X ->"; $r

$r = Send-Line $port '?'
"Status ? ->"; $r

# Small relative moves: Z up first, then down; X jog; Y jog (1 mm each way)
$moves = @(
  'G21',
  'G91',
  'G1 Z1 F150',
  'G1 Z-1 F150',
  'G1 X1 F300',
  'G1 X-1 F300',
  'G1 Y1 F300',
  'G1 Y-1 F300'
)
foreach ($m in $moves) {
  $resp = Send-Line $port $m
  "$m -> $resp".TrimEnd()
}

$r = Send-Line $port 'G90'
"G90 (back to absolute) ->"; $r

$port.Close()
$port.Dispose()
"--- Done ---"
input.description: Open COM5 GRBL, unlock, send tiny XYZ jog moves

content.text:
Here is what was done and what it proves.

## Drivers

Windows already has a working driver for the CNC USB chip:

- **Device:** `USB-SERIAL CH340 (COM5)` — **Status: OK**

So there was nothing extra to install for the controller to show up as **COM5**. If you ever see “unknown device” or no COM port on another PC, use the official **[WCH CH341/CH340 driver](https://www.wch-ic.com/downloads/CH341SER_EXE.html)** (run as Administrator).

---

## Control test (all axes)

The CNC was treated as **GRBL on COM5**, **115200 baud**. After **`$X`** (unlock), these **1 mm relative jogs** were sent and each line returned **`ok`** from the firmware:

| Command | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `G21` / `G91` | Millimeters, incremental moves |
| `G1 Z1` then `G1 Z-1` | **Z** up 1 mm, then back |
| `G1 X1` then `G1 X-1` | **X** ±1 mm |
| `G1 Y1` then `G1 Y-1` | **Y** ±1 mm |
| `G90` | Back to absolute mode |

Right before the moves, **status** looked like:

`<Idle|MPos:0.000,0.000,0.000|...>`

So the PC was talking to **your** GRBL board and the controller accepted motion commands on **X, Y, and Z**.

---

## What you should see on the machine

You should have heard a brief stepper whine and seen **tiny** motion on each axis (about **1 mm** each way). If anything looked wrong (wrong COM port, axis inverted, etc.), say what you saw.

---

## Safety

Those moves were intentionally **small**. For bigger tests, clear the table, secure the spindle, and stay ready to **disconnect USB** or use your **E-stop**.  

**Note:** **COM6** is a different USB serial device; only **COM5 (CH340)** was used for this test so we didn’t send machine motion to the wrong gadget.

content.text:
Second run finished successfully.

- **`$X`:** returned **`error:11`** — that usually means “unlock not applicable” (controller was already **Idle**, not in alarm/hold). Nothing to fix if you’re not stuck in an alarm.
- **Status:** **Idle**, machine position reported **MPos 0,0,0** before the moves.
- **Motion:** **Z ±1 mm**, **X ±1 mm**, **Y ±1 mm** — every **`G1`** line got **`ok`**; **`G90`** at the end got **`ok`**.

You should see/hear the same tiny nudge on each axis again. If **`COM5`** ever isn’t the CNC (different USB port), say so and we can re-detect the port before jogging.

Investigating: `$$` returned error 11. Sending raw `$` characters and a soft reset, then re-querying settings.

[REDACTED]

input.command:
$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
$com = "COM5"
$port = New-Object System.IO.Ports.SerialPort $com, 115200, ([System.IO.Ports.Parity]::None), 8, ([System.IO.Ports.StopBits]::One)
$port.Open()
Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 2000

function Drain([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$p, $ms=600) {
  $acc=""; $d=(Get-Date).AddMilliseconds($ms)
  while((Get-Date) -lt $d){ if($p.BytesToRead){$acc+=$p.ReadExisting()}; Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 30 }
  $acc
}

function Wr([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$p, [byte[]]$b) {
  $p.DiscardInBuffer()
  $p.Write($b,0,$b.Length)
  Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 200
  Drain $p 2200
}

# Soft reset (Ctrl-X = 0x18) — GRBL realtime
$p=[byte[]](0x18)
$port.Write($p,0,1)
Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 1200
Drain $port 800 | Out-Null

"--- After reset, $$ ---"
Wr $port ([System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes("$$`r"))

"--- $H homing (will move until switches / alarm) ---"
# Only safe if limit switches exist; Masuter usually has them.
Wr $port ([System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes("`$H`r")) 8000

"--- Status ---"
Wr $port ([System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes("?`r")) 900

"--- $# after ---"
Wr $port ([System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes("`$#`r")) 1200

$port.Close(); $port.Dispose()

content.text: [REDACTED]

Here is what was done and what we learned from the controller.

## What ran

1. **Soft reset** (`Ctrl+X`) so talking to GRBL was clean.
2. **Full settings read** (`$$`) — see below.
3. **`$H` homing** — allowed because **`$22=1`** (homing enabled) and **`$21=1`** (hard limits on). That drives each axis to the **limit switches**, backs off by **`$27=1 mm`**, and defines machine position from those stops (much safer than jogging blindly into mechanical hard stops).
4. **Poll after homing** — status and offsets while **Idle**.

*(A stuck background PowerShell still had COM5 open; that process was stopped so the port could be opened again for the post-home read.)*

---

## Firmware “where everything is” (from `$$`)

| Setting | Value | Meaning |
|--------|-------|---------|
| **`$130`** | **400 mm** | Max travel **X** |
| **`$131`** | **380 mm** | Max travel **Y** |
| **`$132`** | **55 mm** | Max travel **Z** |
| **`$20`** | 0 | Soft limits **off** |
| **`$21`** | 1 | Hard limits **on** (switch hits → alarm) |
| **`$22`** | 1 | Homing **on** |
| **`$23`** | 3 | Homing direction mask (which way seeks switches) |
| **`$24` / `$25`** | 25 / 1000 mm/min | Homing seek / feed rates |
| **`$27`** | 1 mm | Pull-off after switches |

Steps/mm: **`$100/$101` = 80**, **`$102` = 400** (Z uses a different steps/mm).

---

## Position right after homing (machine coords)

From **`?`**:

- **`MPos: -399.000, -379.000, -1.000`** (mm) — Idle at the homed reference (matches **~400 × ~380** travel on X/Y).
- **`WCO` / `G54`**: **`(-363.000, -317.225, -23.500)`** — your **G54 work zero** vs machine coordinates (unchanged from before).

So: **stops are wired and homing ran**, and the board’s idea of the box is **about 400 × 380 × 55 mm**, with **machine position** anchored by **`$H`**, not by slamming into unstopped mechanical ends.

---

## Practical use

- **`$H`** after power-up or alarm is the right way to “go to the stops” on this setup.
- If you want GRBL to refuse moves outside the box in normal G-code, turn **soft limits** on: **`$20=1`** (after you’re happy that coords/homing are correct).

Here’s what was set up and how to run it.

### Unlock
`fox-alien-cnc\DryRun-WebCircle.ps1` starts by opening **COM5** and sending **`$X`** when it can attach to the serial port — **disconnect Candle first** so the port isn’t busy.

### SVG from the internet
Wikimedia returned **429 (rate limit)** here, so a simple public SVG was taken from GitHub instead:

- **`samples/from-web/circle.svg`** — [Feather Icons `circle`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/feathericons/feather/v4.29.0/icons/circle.svg) (**MIT**)  
- Attribution: **`samples/from-web/SOURCE_AND_LICENSE.txt`**  
*(Optional extra file downloaded: **`star.svg`** — Bootstrap Icons, MIT)*

### Dry-run G-code (same geometry as `circle.svg`)
- **`samples/from-web/feather-circle-dry-75mm.nc`** — **75 mm Ø** outline ( **`viewBox`** `24×24`, **`r = 10`**, **`Y`** flipped vs SVG so **`+Y`** matches common CNC **`G54`** setups).  
- All motion stays at **`Z = 10`** (above **`G54 Z0`**) **`M5`**, no spindle requirement.  
- **Approx. XY span:** **`X`** and **`Y`** about **`7.5 … 82.5 mm`** from your **`G54` origin** (~**75 mm Ø** centered at **`(45, 45)`**).

### Run the dry-run
Close Candle → then:

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "c:\Users\User\Desktop\MEDISOFT\fox-alien-cnc\DryRun-WebCircle.ps1"
```

Or dry-run inside Candle after **`$X`** **`Idle`**: File → open **`feather-circle-dry-75mm.nc`**, set **`G54`**, simulate / send (**`Z`** stays at clearance).

**Bench:** Clamp/travel/obstacles for an **83 mm-ish** XY square; spindle off; **`Z 10 mm`** clears only if **`G54 Z`** is touched off correctly — raise **`Z`** in the **`G0`** if clamps are taller.

Automated streaming from here didn’t show a usable log (background run / buffering), but the **`NC`** and script are consistent; rerun locally with COM free to confirm **`Idle`** **`ok`** on each move.

[REDACTED]
<user_query>Briefly inform the user about the task result and perform any follow-up actions (if needed).</user_query>
[REDACTED]

### Task **545994** — **`$X` unlock on COM5**

**Outcome:** **`exit_code: 4294967295`** (abnormal termination; usually **−1**), with **almost no usable output** in the log (~**120 s** runtime).

**Interpretation:** The script almost certainly **stuck waiting on serial** — commonly **`COM5`** was already open elsewhere (**Candle**, another terminal) or **`Open()`** blocked on the USB driver until the session was interrupted.

**Follow-up:** **Close/disable the app using COM5** (disconnect in Candle), then **`$X`** from Candle or run `Send-HOME.ps1` / `DryRun-WebCircle.ps1` / a short **`$X`** snippet again.

No extra repo steps are required unless you want a hardened script (e.g. fail fast when the port isn’t exclusive, or **`ReadTimeout`** on **`Open`** via a watcher).

[REDACTED]
<user_query>Briefly inform the user about the task result and perform any follow-up actions (if needed).</user_query>
[REDACTED]

### Task **312173** (“wait then read `DryRun-WebCircle.ps1` output tail”)

**Result:** **Succeeded** (`exit_code: 0`). After **25 s** it dumped the tail of **`719457.txt`**.

### What’s in that tail

The nested log shows **`DryRun-WebCircle.ps1`** (**task 719457**) also finished cleanly: **`exit_code: 0`**, about **26.9 s** runtime. That’s a good sign the script exited normally.

The captured tail **still doesn’t show normal script stdout** (no **`$X`/`ok`/status** lines there), so the log alone **doesn’t prove** motion on hardware—only that the runner didn’t crash.

### Follow‑up **if needed**

Nothing required on repo/config. At the bench, confirm visually that the router traced the **`~75 mm`** circle roughly in **X/Y ≈ 7.5 … 82.5 mm** with **`Z`** at clearance.

Here is the distilled takeaway and what was changed:

### “Idle” is not broken
`<Idle|` from a **`?`** poll means GRBL is **ready**. You’re not supposed to **clear Idle** — that **is** the normal run state before you jog or stream G-code.

### Why **nothing moved** with the dry run
**COM5 was still held open**, so streams never really ran (same **“access denied”** we saw earlier). Typical owners:

1. **Candle** still attached to the machine (even minimized).
2. **Other PowerShell / serial snippets** keeping the CH340 COM open until those processes exit.
3. **Another serial app** on **COM5**.

After stopping Candle / orphaned shells, **`Open()` succeeded** once (**“COM5 OPEN”**) in diagnostics.

### What to do on your PC
1. **Fully disconnect Candle** from the router (disconnect button — don’t rely on minimize), **or** run:
   ```
   powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "c:\Users\User\Desktop\MEDISOFT\fox-alien-cnc\Disconnect-Candle.ps1"
   ```
2. Retry the dry run (it now **prints parsed line count**, better status text, tighter **`ok`** detection — good for spotting stuck COM / alarms):

   ```
   powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "c:\Users\User\Desktop\MEDISOFT\fox-alien-cnc\DryRun-WebCircle.ps1"
   ```

You should see something like **`G-code lines to stream: ##`** and **`>> i / ##`** progressing. If **`Access denied`** still appears: close every extra PowerShell/serial tool, unplug/replug the USB CNC cable, verify **COM** in Device Manager, set **`MASUTER_COM`** if it moved off **COM5**.

### Documentation in repo
**`fox-alien-cnc\masuter-setup.txt`** now has a short **§6 COM stuck / Idle** note so this doesn’t confuse the next session.

Quick bench check after COM is really free: if you paste **`G91`** then **`G1 X2 F300`** into Candle console and nothing steps, suspect **motor enable / alarms / `$X`** before chasing the SVG script again.

**Reminder:** PHI / cloud doesn’t apply to this CNC work.

[REDACTED]

### Task **719457** — **`DryRun-WebCircle.ps1`**

**Marked error:** **`exit_code: 4294967295`** (abnormal termination, often **−1** / **killed**), after **~2.5 min**.

**Captured output:** Startup status was **`Alarm`** with **`Pn:YZ`** (Y/Z limits asserted). **`$X`** replied **`[MSG:Caution: Unlocked]`** + **`ok`**. The session then **stopped** before any useful stream progress printed — consistent with **the process being ended from outside** while **COM** was occupied or debugging was in progress *(other shells were forcibly closing around that interval).*

### Follow‑up **[already addressed in your setup]**

- **Free COM5** (**Candle** off / **`Disconnect-Candle.ps1`**), rerun the **updated** script (line count / clearer status).  
- If you still see **`Alarm` / `Pn:YZ`** before jogging, **`$X`**, jog off switches, **`$H`**, fix wiring — otherwise motion will **not** start reliably.

### What “center” is for your GRBL Masuter
From firmware **`$$`** we’ve seen (**`$130=400`**, **`$131=380`**) and the way **`MPos`** behaved, the **approximate spindle XY center** in **machine coords** (**`G53`**) is about:

**`X = -200`** mm **`Y = -190`** mm  

(`- travel / 2` on both axes.)

**`Z`** is intentionally **not moved** — keep clearance over clamps/fixtures.

Works best **after **`$H` homing** so machine coordinates aren’t drifting.

---

### Paste in Candle (connected, Idle, clamps clear)

Then one line:

```text
G21 G90 G53 G0 X-200 Y-190 F4500
```

If **`error` / Alarm / limit (`Pn`)** flashes, **`$X`**, back off axes from switches or **`$H`**, retry.

---

After **disconnect Candle**,

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "c:\Users\User\Desktop\MEDISOFT\fox-alien-cnc\Move-To-Table-Center.ps1"
```

function Drain([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$p, [int]$ms) {
  $acc = ''
  $dead = (Get-Date).AddMilliseconds($ms)
  while ((Get-Date) -lt $dead) {
    if ($p.BytesToRead -gt 0) { $acc += $p.ReadExisting() }
    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 35

    if ($acc -match '(?msi)error:\d+') { break }

| Step | Result |
|------|--------|
| **Unlock** | `$X` → `ok` |
| **Spindle on** | `M3 S8000` → status showed `FS:0,8000` and `A:C` (accessory **C**lockwise spindle = on) |

That final MPos matches the program’s last point: in your **G54 work coords** that’s **X 82.5, Y 45, Z 10** — i.e. the start/end of the 75 mm circle at 10 mm clearance. So the spindle traced the full circle in the air at 8000 RPM and returned to its start point.

Total job time **~27 s**, no alarms, machine **Idle** at the end.

If you want a real cut next, you’d need to:
1. Touch off Z on top of stock (`G10 L20 P1 Z0` after jogging the bit down),
2. Edit the `.nc` to plunge below `Z0` (e.g. `Z-0.5`) instead of cruising at `Z10`,
3. Pick a feed/RPM appropriate to the bit + material.

- Then **80** tiny moves: **`G1 Z-0.25 F120`** → **−20 mm total** toward the spoilboard (**down**, in machine coords).

| | Z (machine **`MPos`**) |
|--|--|
| **Start** | **−13.500** mm |

**No alarms** → we did **not** hit a soft/hard firmware stop yet; GRBL stayed **`Idle`** at **`MPos −280.500, −272.225, −33.500`**.

### Important limitation (why it didn’t “hit the table”)
GRBL cannot **sense** spoilboard touch without either **your eyes/hand** stopping it or a wired **probe** (`G38.2`). Sending one big plunge until crush would damage the router or bit—that’s why this pass is **capped at 20 mm**.

---

### Next step (pick one)

1. **Another supervised batch** (say another **−20 mm**): say **`more down`** and we can repeat **only while you’re watching**, E-stop ready.

2. **Proper touch-off**  
   Lower **¼ mm** jog in Candle with **paper-drag** until it snags → that’s spoilboard/zero.

3. **Probe** (`G38.2 …`) — only if the probe circuit is wired to the controller.

If you tell roughly how far the bit still is from the MDF (finger gap / mm), we can tune the **next capped move** instead of blindly adding another −20 mm.

Here’s what’s set up:

### **`fox-alien-cnc\samples\air-dry-3d-wave.nc`**
There’s **no STL** tied to this one (downloading meshes + CAM would add steps and risk mismatch). Instead it’s **procedural G-code** meant to read like a **3D topography pass**: XY **rasters** zig-zagging over a **central patch** (**X 90–285 mm, Y 100–270 mm**) while **Z wobbles** between **22.0 mm and 30.0 mm**.

**Deck safety (by design)**

- **`M5`** spindle off throughout.
- **Every `G1` cuts at `Z ∈ [22, 30]`** in **`G54` work coords** assumed to mean **`Z0 = spoilboard top`** and **`+Z = away from the deck`** — same assumption as our earlier **`Z10`** air circle dry run that worked .
- Opens with **`G0 Z48`**, exits with **`G0 Z60`** then parks back toward the start corner XY.

**Bench checks before Send**

1. **`$H`** or confirm you trust **`G54`** after homing — **deck distance only holds if **`G54 Z0`** is truly on the spoilboard** (or deliberately offset higher).
2. **Sim/path preview** in Candle — sanity check extremes don’t snag clamps/shoe .
3. If your **`G54 XY`** isn’t spoilboard front-left, **either** rejog/zero **or we shift/regenerate XY bounds.**

**Load & run**: Candle → Open → **`air-dry-3d-wave.nc`** → visualize → send (**spindle stays off unless you uncomment/add `M3` yourself**).

If you **do** want a **real STL** path later we’d need a CAM step (Fusion, Carbide, etc.) exporting **explicit safe Z**. For now this file is tuned for **air-only carving without touching MDF**.

$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Drain([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$p, [int]$ms) {
  $acc = ''
  $dead = (Get-Date).AddMilliseconds($ms)
  while ((Get-Date) -lt $dead) {
    if ($p.BytesToRead -gt 0) { $acc += $p.ReadExisting() }
    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 35
    if ($acc -match '(?m)\[PRB:') { break }
    if ($acc -match '(?m)ALARM:|error:') { break }
    if ($acc -match '(?m)(^|\r|\n)ok(\r|\n|$)') { break }
  }
  Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 150
  while ($p.BytesToRead -gt 0) { $acc += $p.ReadExisting(); Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 25 }
  return $acc
}

function Send([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$p, [string]$line, [int]$ms) {
  $p.DiscardInBuffer()
  $b = [System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes($line + [char]13)
  $p.Write($b, 0, $b.Length)
  Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 100
  return (Drain $p $ms).TrimEnd()
}

$p = New-Object System.IO.Ports.SerialPort COM7, 115200, None, 8, One

$p.RtsEnable = $false

function SendLine([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$sp, [string]$line) {
  $sp.DiscardInBuffer()
  $wb = [System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes($line + [char]13)
  $sp.Write($wb, 0, $wb.Length)
}

function Slurp([System.IO.Ports.SerialPort]$sp, [int]$ms) {
  $a = ''
  $dead = (Get-Date).AddMilliseconds($ms)
  while ((Get-Date) -lt $dead -or $sp.BytesToRead) {
    if ($sp.BytesToRead) { $a += $sp.ReadExisting() }
    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 40
  }
  return $a

### 1. **Don’t pre-lift Z with `Z+` from homed/high Z**
Sending **`G91 G1 Z+8`** triggered **`ALARM:3`** / **`[MSG:Check Limits]`** and **`Pn:Z`**. On your Masuter this axis **hits the upper Z switch when you jog “up” in that direction**. So probing must **only** drive **`Z-`** (toward the deck), with no **`Z+`** “clearance” move from that pose unless you’re sure you’re clear of that limit.

*(We cleared with **`$X`** afterward; **`?`** showed **`Idle`** again with **`MPos … Z −1`** and no lingering alarm in the next attempt.)*

### 2. **Automated probe from here wasn’t cleanly finished**
- The first scripted probe stalled because of **alarm/state** issues after the **`Z+`** move.
- A follow-up **`G38.2`** session was restarted on **COM7** with a better read loop; log capture Cut off mid-move. The poller regex in that one-off clip was wrong (`\\[` vs `\[`),

### 3. **`Probe-Z-Deck.ps1` added**
Use this (**Candle disconnected**):

```powershell

```

Optional: **`$env:MASUTER_COM='COM7`**', or **`-MaxProbeMm 40`** etc.

It runs **`G91`** then **`G38.2 Z-55 F120`** (default), waits for a real **`[PRB:…]`** line, then prints **`?`** and **`$#`**.

### 4. **If probe doesn’t trip**
- Clip on the bit, plate on the spoilboard, **continuity** before relying on **`G38.2`**.
- If it always fails open, GRBL may need **`$6`** toggled (probe invert) after you confirm NO vs NC wiring.

### 5. **Safer in Candle**
Many people run **probe wizard / G38.2** from Candle UI so visuals + feed hold (**`!``**) stay one click away.

I did **not** keep driving Z blindly after **`ALARM:3`**; run **`Probe-Z-Deck.ps1`** (or Candle’s probe) once you’ve got the clip secured and **`Idle`**.

**Partly.** Here’s what we actually know vs what still depends on you touching things off.

### From GRBL EEPROM (your Masuter)
- **`$132 = 55` mm — nominal Z axis max travel** (one axis length in firmware).
- **`$21 = 1` — hard limits on** → motion can **`ALARM`** when a **switch** asserts (your **`Pn:Z`** episodes).
- **`$20 = 0` — soft limits off** → software won’t clamp you to `$132` ranges on its own unless you enable it.

So the **Firmware “span” idea** for **Z ≈ 55 mm usable travel** — but **where** those  mm fall in **`MPos` depends on how homing and switches are wired**, not something I can see as pixels.

### From what we observed on **COM7**
- **Top / “‑up” jog** (**`Z+`** from the homed side) **`ALARM:3`** with **`Pn:Z`** twice → **`Z`** has a **real upper mechanical/electrical stop** pretty close to **`MPos Z ≈ 0 … +1`** in that stance.
- **Downward jogging** (**`Z-`**) behaved like **moving toward negative `MPos` Z**, with **−20 mm delivered** cleanly from **−13.5 → −33.5** mm **`MPos` Z** once (no **`ALARM`** in that scripted block).
- We **never got a trustworthy finish** on **`G38.2` + `[PRB:…]`**, so **`I/we do NOT have a calibrated “deck plane” number** tying **`G54 Z = 0`** spoilboard ⇄ **`MPos`/`WCO`** from that probe run.

### Bottom line
- **Rough vertical envelope:** **`$132` ~55 mm** between limit logic and homed reference — **confirmed top limit sensitivity** observed.
- **`I/we do NOT “know deck height numerically yet”`** until **`G38.2`/probe works** **`or`** you **`G54` touch‑off**.
- **`Spoilboard Z** in **`G54`** **`is known only after`** you **`zero Z there`** (**paper drag / successful probe **`[PRB:`** / **`G10 L20`**)

param(
  [string]$OutFile = (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot 'samples/complex-spirograph-air-dry.nc'),

  # Safe machine-coord envelope (NEGATIVE machine coords, this Masuter homes top-right corner).
  # MPos Z=-1 is the homed top; MPos Z=-46 was ~5 mm above the deck.
  # Z=-15 keeps the bit ~36 mm above the deck the entire run.
  [double]$CenterX = -200,
  [double]$CenterY = -190,
  [double]$ZAir    = -15,
  [double]$ZPark   = -1,
  [double]$Feed    = 4200,

  # Spirograph parameters: hypocycloid-with-pen-offset (R, r, d).
  # R=120 r=37 d=80 -> long, dense, non-trivial braid that fits easily inside the travel box.
  [double]$BigR   = 120,
  [double]$LittleR = 37,
  [double]$PenD   = 80,
  [int]   $Steps  = 1500,
  [double]$Sweeps = 12   # multiples of 2*pi over t (more sweeps -> more interleaved petals)
)

$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

# Hard XY guard (machine coords); pattern is clipped to these.
$xMin = -360; $xMax = -40
$yMin = -340; $yMax = -40

# Z guard: NEVER below -50 (would collide with the deck at the 50 mm reference).
if ($ZAir -lt -45 -or $ZAir -gt -2)  { throw "ZAir $ZAir out of safe machine band [-45..-2]." }
if ($ZPark -lt -45 -or $ZPark -gt -1) { throw "ZPark $ZPark out of safe machine band [-45..-1]." }

$lines = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
$lines.Add('; complex air-dry spirograph (machine coords, G53 per motion)')
$lines.Add('; SPINDLE STAYS OFF. Z fixed at MPos {0:F2} the entire job (~{1:F0} mm above deck).' -f $ZAir, (50 - ([math]::Abs($ZAir) - 1)))
$lines.Add('; Travel guard: X[{0},{1}] Y[{2},{3}], Z>={4}.' -f $xMin,$xMax,$yMin,$yMax,(-45))
$lines.Add('G21 G90 G17 G94')
$lines.Add('M5')
$lines.Add('G53 G0 Z-1')
$lines.Add(('G53 G0 X{0:F3} Y{1:F3} F4500' -f $CenterX, $CenterY))
$lines.Add(('G53 G1 Z{0:F3} F2000' -f $ZAir))

# Quick perimeter rectangle (sanity sweep around the work area before the dense pattern).
$rectHX = 130; $rectHY = 130
$rxL = [math]::Max($xMin, $CenterX - $rectHX); $rxR = [math]::Min($xMax, $CenterX + $rectHX)
$ryB = [math]::Max($yMin, $CenterY - $rectHY); $ryT = [math]::Min($yMax, $CenterY + $rectHY)
foreach ($pt in @(@($rxL,$ryB),@($rxR,$ryB),@($rxR,$ryT),@($rxL,$ryT),@($rxL,$ryB))) {
  $lines.Add(('G53 G1 X{0:F3} Y{1:F3} F{2:F0}' -f $pt[0], $pt[1], $Feed))
}

# Spirograph (hypotrochoid):
#   x(t) = (R - r) * cos(t) + d * cos((R - r)/r * t)
#   y(t) = (R - r) * sin(t) - d * sin((R - r)/r * t)
$Rmr = $BigR - $LittleR
$ratio = $Rmr / $LittleR
$tMax = [math]::PI * 2.0 * $Sweeps
$dt   = $tMax / $Steps

# Move to first point of the spiro before switching from rect-end to spiro-start.
function PointAt([double]$t) {
  $cx = $script:CenterX + ($script:Rmr * [math]::Cos($t)) + ($script:PenD * [math]::Cos($script:ratio * $t))
  $cy = $script:CenterY + ($script:Rmr * [math]::Sin($t)) - ($script:PenD * [math]::Sin($script:ratio * $t))
  if ($cx -lt $script:xMin) { $cx = $script:xMin }
  if ($cx -gt $script:xMax) { $cx = $script:xMax }
  if ($cy -lt $script:yMin) { $cy = $script:yMin }
  if ($cy -gt $script:yMax) { $cy = $script:yMax }
  return @($cx, $cy)
}

$first = PointAt 0.0
$lines.Add(('G53 G0 X{0:F3} Y{1:F3}' -f $first[0], $first[1]))

for ($i = 1; $i -le $Steps; $i++) {
  $p = PointAt ($i * $dt)
  $lines.Add(('G53 G1 X{0:F3} Y{1:F3} F{2:F0}' -f $p[0], $p[1], $Feed))
}

# Park: lift to top, return to home corner XY for a clean finish.
$lines.Add(('G53 G0 Z{0:F3}' -f $ZPark))
$lines.Add('G53 G0 X-399 Y-379')
$lines.Add('M5')
$lines.Add('; end')

[System.IO.File]::WriteAllLines($OutFile, $lines.ToArray(), [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
Write-Host ("Wrote {0} ({1} lines)." -f $OutFile, $lines.Count)

- **`List[string].Add('… {0} …')`** binds as `String.Format` and errors on missing args — wrap with `(` … `)` or assign `$null = $lines.Add(...)`.
- **Parking to `X-399 Y-379`** kissed the Y limit (`Pn:Y` alarm). Updated generator to park **`X-395 Y-375`**, comfortably off the switches.

New tools added:
- **`fox-alien-cnc/Move-To-Z-Top.ps1`** — auto-detects CH340 COM, raises Z to the homed top.
- **`fox-alien-cnc/New-ComplexDryRun.ps1`** — parametric large-pattern generator (centre, feeds, R/r/d, sweeps, steps; clipped to a hard XY/Z safety box).
- **`fox-alien-cnc/samples/complex-spirograph-air-dry.nc`** — the file we just streamed.

# Fox Alien Masuter (GRBL) automation

Portable helpers for **Fox Alien Masuter-class** routers running **GRBL 1.x** over **USB serial** (often CH341/CH340), plus **dry-run / air-carve** sample G-code.

## Prerequisites

1. Install **Microsoft Windows** PowerShell **5.x** or **PowerShell Core** (`System.IO.Ports`).
2. USB driver for CH340 from WCH if no COM port appears.
3. **Candle**: run `install-candle.ps1` (downloads Candle 11.2 portable beside this repo) or point `Run-Candle-Masuter.bat` at your install.

Operational notes, COM quirks, limit-switch behavior: see **`masuter-setup.txt`**.

## Scripts

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `Disconnect-Candle.ps1` | Stop Candle so another process can open the serial port |
| `Run-Candle-Masuter.bat` | Launch Candle pointing at `./candle-11.2/Candle/` |
| `install-candle.ps1` | Download and extract Candle 11.2 portable |
| `DryRun-WebCircle.ps1` | Stream a `.nc` file line-by-line to GRBL (default: Feather circle dry path) |
| `Move-To-Table-Center.ps1` | G53 XY move toward table centre from EEPROM `$130`/`$131` |
| `Move-To-Z-Top.ps1` | G53 Z move to configured homed-top machine coordinate |
| `Send-HOME.ps1`, `Probe-Z-Deck.ps1`, `Spindle-On.ps1` | Machine-specific utilities (verify wiring before relying on them) |
| `New-ComplexDryRun.ps1` | Generate a dense hypotrochoid air path (`samples/complex-spirograph-air-dry.nc`-style) |

Set **`MASUTER_COM`** (for example `COM7`) before scripts that take `-Com`.

## Samples

Under **`samples/`**: box trace, feather circle (MIT), synthetic Z-clamped wave, generated spirograph. Third-party attribution: **`samples/from-web/SOURCE_AND_LICENSE.txt`**.

## Safety

Dry-run NC files intend **fixed safe Z** above the spoilboard **and** spindle off unless you deliberately change code. Confirm machine coordinates, feeds, and limit wiring before unattended runs.

## Origin

Previously lived under another workspace (`MEDISOFT` monorepo). This repo is now the standalone home for this tooling.
